commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ef51eb22200252dcc67141a2bc824127c2f52170)
commands.review_summary:
The PR renames Gateway chat-history final-budget accounting from placeholder counting to omitted-message counting, adds front byte-cap drops to the truncation log count, and updates focused budget tests.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +25. Total +36 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows the helper drops earlier messages while returning zero, and the Gateway caller logs truncation only when the combined count is positive.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining actions are contributor-owned real behavior proof and normal maintainer review of diagnostic count semantics, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(gateway): report omitted chat-history messages in truncation log
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`enforceChatHistoryFinalBudget` (the final byte-budget step of the `chat.history` Gateway response) returned `placeholderCount: 0` in the branch that keeps **only the last message** while silently dropping every earlier one:

```ts
const last = messages.at(-1);
if (last && jsonUtf8Bytes([last]) <= maxBytes) {
  return { messages: [last], placeholderCount: 0 }; // dropped N-1 messages, reported 0
}
```

The caller gates its truncation log on that count:

```ts
if (placeholderCount > 0) {
  logLargePayload({ surface: "gateway.chat.history", action: "truncated", ... });
}
```

So whenever history was trimmed down to its last message, the count was `0`, the gate never fired, and the truncation was invisible to operators. Closes #96783.

## Why This Change Was Made

The field was named/used inconsistently: the helper computed it as "number of placeholder messages inserted", but the caller treats it as a generic "did we omit history → log it" signal (it sums it with `replaceOversizedChatHistoryMessages`'s `replacedCount`). The drop-to-last branch omits real history yet inserts no placeholder, so the literal `0` was correct for "placeholders" but wrong for "omissions".

Two adjacent omission sources also went uncounted: the front byte cap (`capArrayByJsonBytes`) drops the oldest messages and returns no count, so that loss was never logged either.

## User Impact

Operators now get the `payload.large` / `truncated` diagnostic (and the debug line) whenever `chat.history` discards older history for any reason — drop-to-last, placeholder replacement, or front byte-cap trimming. No API/response shape change for clients; this is purely diagnostic accounting. The renamed field is internal (only the helper, its single Gateway caller, and the focused test referenced it; the TUI/embedded-stub callers only read `messages`).

## Evidence

**Before (current `main`)** — `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`: the keep-last branch returns `placeholderCount: 0` despite dropping `messages.length - 1` messages, so `if (placeholderCount > 0)` never logs.

**After** — `enforceChatHistoryFinalBudget` returns `omittedCount` and every omitting branch reports the number of input messages that lost their verbatim representation:
- within budget / empty → `0`
- keep only last → `messages.length - 1`
- last replaced by placeholder / sentinel → `messages.length`

The caller also adds the front byte-cap drop count (`replaced.messages.length - capped.length`) so all omission sources feed the truncation log.

**Focused regression test** (`src/gateway/server-methods/chat-history-budget.test.ts`) exercises the real exported function over actual over-budget inputs:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat-history-budget.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
```

New/updated cases: drop-to-last now asserts `omittedCount === 1`, a multi-drop case asserts `=== 2`, and a multi-message placeholder case asserts the whole list is counted as omitted. Formatting verified with `oxfmt`.

AI-assisted.
target.author: zengwen-dt
